info.name: Comprobify API — Internal (Admin)
info.description:
Comprobify internal/admin collection. Flow:
1. Publish Agreements (TERMS, PRIVACY, DPA)
2. Register or Create Tenant + Create Issuer + Create API Key
3. Verify tenant (admin or email)
4. Tenant creates a subscription → payment_id captured
5. List Payments → review queue
6. Get Payment Proof → view file
7. Review Payment (VERIFIED)
8. Self-bill invoice through own issuer → capture accessKey
9. Link Invoice to Subscription → awaits SRI authorization
10. Run Subscription Jobs (for tier-changes and renewals)

variable.description: Base URL of the API (no trailing slash)
Bearer API key issued for the tenant (from Register or POST /v1/keys)
Admin secret (ADMIN_SECRET env var on the server)
49-digit SRI access key — copy from Create Invoice response
Issuer ID — copy from List Issuers or Create Branch response. Used as X-Issuer-Id header on document requests.
API key ID — copy from List Keys response (used for revocation)
Tenant ID — copy from Create Tenant (admin) response
Subscription ID — copy from Create Subscription response
Payment ID — copy from Create Subscription response
Notification ID — copy from List Notifications response (used for Mark as Read)
Webhook endpoint ID — copy from Register Webhook response
Version string of the currently published TERMS document — captured from GET /v1/agreements
Email verification token — copy from the verification email link (?token=...)

request.description: Public, no authentication required. Returns the full tier catalog (FREE/STARTER/GROWTH/BUSINESS) — quota, monthly and yearly price, overage rate, document types, branch/webhook limits, and rate limits — for a pricing page to render without hardcoding the same numbers a second time.

item.name: Registration
item.description: Public registration and email verification endpoints. No authentication required.

formdata.description: Optional trade name
Optional main (fiscal) address
Optional company logo for RIDE PDFs. PNG recommended (transparent background). Max 500 KB. Recommended: 600x170 px, landscape (~3.5:1 ratio).
Optional special taxpayer resolution number
Optional branch address
Optional. Document type codes to enable (default: ["01"])
Optional. Starting sequential numbers per document type. Any type not listed defaults to 1.
Optional. Email language: es (default) or en.
Optional. Frontend URL for the email verification link. Token appended as ?token=<token>. https required in production.

request.description:
Self-service registration. Creates a tenant, issuer, and sandbox API key in one call.

Store the returned `apiKey` — it is shown only once. Copy it into the `api_key` collection variable.

If the email already exists and is not suspended, the endpoint revokes the current sandbox key and returns a new one (200 with `recovered: true`).

Optional `verificationRedirectUrl` sets a frontend page for the email verification link.
Verify your email address using the token from the registration email. Required before promoting to production.

Resends the verification email. Generates a fresh token (invalidating the previous one) and resets the expiry.

Two rate limits apply:
- IP-based: shared with POST /v1/register — 5 requests per hour per IP.
- Per-account cooldown: 60 seconds between resends for the same email. Returns 429 if not elapsed.

Returns the same generic message whether or not the email exists (no enumeration).

item.name: Tenants
item.description: Tenant settings and lifecycle management. Require a Bearer API key.

request.description: Resolves the tenant owning the authenticated API key (id, email, subscriptionTier, status, documentCount, documentQuota, sandbox). Useful for a third-party app that only has an API key and needs to discover its numeric tenant id.

exec: if (r.tenant?.agreementVersion) console.log('Last accepted agreement version:', r.tenant.agreementVersion);

request.description: Updates the preferred language for the authenticated tenant. Used for all outgoing emails. Supported values: `es` (Spanish, default), `en` (English).

Promotes the entire tenant (all branches) from sandbox to production. This is **one-way** — cannot be reversed.

All active sandbox API keys are revoked automatically. The response includes one new production key per revoked sandbox key (same label). **Store all tokens immediately — they are shown only once.**

`initialSequentials` is optional. If omitted, all active document types on all issuers start at sequential 1. Pass `issuerId` + `documentType` + `sequential` for each combination you want to pre-seed.

Requires the tenant's email to be verified (status ACTIVE).

**`tier` and `billingInterval` are optional.** Omit them (or pass `tier` as absent) to stay on FREE in production — promotion never waits on payment. If a paid tier is requested, this also kicks off the subscription pipeline (same as Admin's Create Subscription) and the response includes `subscription`, `payment`, and `bankTransfer` alongside `apiKeys`. The tier/quota upgrade itself only lands once that subscription is paid and its self-billed invoice is SRI-authorized — see Submit Payment Proof and Admin > Review Payment.

**If you already started a subscription pre-promotion** via the tenant-facing Create Subscription request (works in sandbox) and it's already ACTIVE, `tier`/`billingInterval` here are ignored entirely — the response surfaces that existing subscription instead of starting a new one.

exec: if (r.agreements.needsAcceptance) console.log('Re-acceptance needed for:', r.agreements.outdated.map(d => d.documentType));

request.description: Check if any document was republished since the tenant last accepted. Use outdated[].url to show updated content.
Record re-acceptance of all currently published documents. Use when agreements returns needsAcceptance: true.
Lists all personalized document instances for this tenant across all versions. status: PENDING (not yet accepted) or ACCEPTED.

Returns the tenant's personalized Terms of Service as HTML — their businessName and RUC substituted in, with the disclaimer notice prepended. Also try /PRIVACY and /DPA. Check response headers: X-Document-Status, X-Template-Version, X-Accepted-At.
Returns the tenant's personalized Privacy Policy as HTML.
DPA with the tenant's own businessName and RUC in the intro paragraph. Verify the substitution is correct before accepting.

Tenant-authenticated (your own API key, not admin_secret). Upload proof of the SPI bank transfer (PNG/JPEG/GIF/PDF, max 2 MB) for a payment you created via promotion, Create Subscription, Change Tier, or a renewal reminder. Moves the payment to REPORTED. Fails if the payment isn't yours, or has already been VERIFIED/REJECTED.

Emails the operator (if ADMIN_NOTIFICATION_EMAIL is configured) that a proof is waiting. After this, the operator reviews the proof (Admin > Review Payment) and verifies or rejects it — you'll get an email either way.

Tenant-authenticated (your own API key). Starts a paid subscription on your own, independent of promotion — works while still in sandbox (no need to promote to production first) or any time after. Requires a verified email, same gate POST /v1/tenants/promote uses.

Same manual proof/review pipeline as the rest of the subscription system: upload proof via Submit Payment Proof, the provider reviews it and links the self-billed invoice, and the tier/quota lands once that invoice is SRI-authorized.

If this subscription is already ACTIVE by the time you call Promote to Production, that call detects it automatically and skips tier selection — any tier/billingInterval you pass there is ignored, and its response surfaces this subscription instead of starting a new one.

Fails with 409 SUBSCRIPTION_ALREADY_IN_FLIGHT if you already have one in progress, or 403 EMAIL_VERIFICATION_REQUIRED if your email isn't verified yet.

Tenant-authenticated (your own API key). Returns your full subscription history, newest first, each with its payments nested (status, amount, rejection_reason if rejected). The proof file itself is never included — only filename/mime type.

This is how you find out a paid-tier upgrade landed — there's no notification; `subscriptionTier`/`documentQuota` on `GET /v1/tenants/me` update automatically once it does, but this endpoint shows the in-between states (and the `rejection_reason` if a proof was rejected, so you know what to fix before re-uploading via Submit Payment Proof).

Tenant-authenticated (your own API key). Changes the tier on your existing ACTIVE subscription — requires having already promoted with a paid tier (see Promote to Production / Admin > Create Subscription). No payment gateway exists, so this rides the same manual proof/review pipeline as the initial subscription rather than charging anything automatically.

**Upgrade** (target tier's price higher than your current one): takes effect immediately once paid. The response includes a prorated `payment` (the price difference scaled by the remaining time in your current billing period) and `bankTransfer` instructions — upload proof via the same Submit Payment Proof endpoint used for your initial subscription. If the prorated amount rounds to $0 (almost no time left in the period), it's applied immediately with no payment step at all.

**Downgrade** (target tier's price lower): scheduled, not immediate, and needs no payment — you already paid for the current period at the higher tier. Your tier/quota stay exactly as they are until `current_period_end`, at which point it's applied automatically by the provider's scheduled job.

Fails with 409 `NO_ACTIVE_SUBSCRIPTION` if you have no ACTIVE subscription, 400 `TIER_CHANGE_NO_OP` if `tier` matches your current one, or 409 `TIER_CHANGE_ALREADY_PENDING` if a change is already in flight (only one at a time).

Poll `GET /v1/subscriptions/me` to see status, and `GET /v1/tenants/me` once it lands.

item.name: API Keys
item.description: Tenant-facing API key management. Mint named keys for each integration, list them, and revoke leaked or unused ones. Require a Bearer API key.

request.description:
Returns every active key for the tenant. The plaintext token is **never** returned — only labels, environments, ids, and creation dates.

Copy a key's `id` into the `api_key_id` variable to use it with Revoke Key.

Creates a new tenant-scoped API key. The plaintext token is shown **once** in the response — store it immediately.

`label` (optional, max 100 chars) — a human-readable name for the integration, e.g. `frontend-prod`, `erp`, `mobile-app`.

`environment` — `"sandbox"` (default) or `"production"`. Production keys can only be minted after the tenant has been promoted to production.

When used on a document request, the key's environment must match the targeted issuer's environment — otherwise the request is rejected with 401.

Marks the key as inactive. The key stops working immediately and cannot be used to authenticate any future request.

You cannot revoke the key you are currently using to make this request — use a different key, or contact admin support.

item.name: Issuers
item.description: Issuer (branch / issue point) management. Require a Bearer API key.

request.description:
Returns all active issuers (branches / issue points) belonging to the authenticated tenant.

Copy an issuer's `id` into the `issuer_id` collection variable to use it as `X-Issuer-Id` on document requests.

exec: if (r.ok && r.issuers?.length) { pm.collectionVariables.set('issuer_id', String(r.issuers[0].id)); console.log('issuer_id set to', r.issuers[0].id); }

request.description: Returns profile information for a single issuer owned by the authenticated tenant: RUC, business name, trade name, branch/issue point codes, and certificate expiry/fingerprint.

formdata.description: Company logo (PNG, JPEG, or GIF; max 500 KB)

request.description: Uploads or replaces the company logo for an issuer. The logo is stored in the database and rendered in the top-left corner of every RIDE PDF generated for that issuer, including PDFs sent as email attachments. Accepted formats: PNG, JPEG, GIF. Max size: 500 KB.

formdata.description: P12/PFX certificate file from an authorized Ecuadorian CA (BANCO CENTRAL or SECURITY DATA)
Optional. Password protecting the P12 file, if any.

request.description: Replaces the P12 certificate (private key + X.509 cert) for an issuer — e.g. when it has expired. Does not affect already-signed documents, since each signed invoice embeds its own copy of the certificate inside the XML signature; only future signing operations use the new certificate.

formdata.description: Optional. Document type codes to enable (default: ["01"])
Optional. Numeric id of the issuer to inherit cert/profile from. Defaults to the tenant's first existing issuer. Ignored if a cert file is uploaded.
Optional — leave blank to reuse the calling issuer certificate

request.description:
Creates a new branch or issue point for the tenant. Inherits RUC, business name, and certificate from an existing issuer of the tenant.

**No new API key is minted** — your existing tenant key already covers every branch via the `X-Issuer-Id` header. New branches inherit the tenant's current environment (sandbox or production).

The cert file and certPassword fields are optional — only provide them if the new branch needs a different certificate.

Tier limits apply: FREE allows 1 branch / 1 issue point, STARTER 3 / 2, GROWTH 10 / 5, BUSINESS unlimited.
Returns the active document types enabled for the specified issuer.
Enables a document type for the specified issuer. If the type was previously removed, it is reactivated. Returns the full updated list of active document types.
Disables a document type for the specified issuer. The last active type cannot be removed.

item.name: Documents
item.description: Document lifecycle endpoints. All requests require a Bearer API key and the `X-Issuer-Id` header identifying the issuing branch.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /v1/issuers)
Optional — unique per intended invoice. Reuse on retries to avoid duplicates.

request.description:
Creates, XSD-validates, and signs a new electronic invoice. Returns the document with status `SIGNED`.

After a successful response, copy the `accessKey` value into the `access_key` collection variable.

The `X-Issuer-Id` header identifies which branch and certificate to use. Get the id from `GET /v1/issuers`.

The `Idempotency-Key` header makes creation idempotent — generate it once per intended invoice and reuse on retries.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /v1/issuers). Must have document type 04 enabled.
Optional — unique per intended credit note. Reuse on retries to avoid duplicates.

request.description:
Creates, XSD-validates, and signs a new credit note referencing a previously authorized invoice. Returns the document with status `SIGNED`.

Unlike Create Invoice, this body has no `payments` block and instead requires `originalDocument` (the invoice being credited — its document type, number, and issue date) plus a `motivo` (reason for the credit note).

The `X-Issuer-Id` header identifies which branch and certificate to use — the issuer must have document type `04` enabled first (see Add Document Type).

The `Idempotency-Key` header makes creation idempotent — generate it once per intended credit note and reuse on retries.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /v1/issuers)

query.description: Filter by status: SIGNED, RECEIVED, RETURNED, AUTHORIZED, NOT_AUTHORIZED
Filter by sequential, contains match against the zero-padded 9-digit value (case-insensitive)
Filter by buyer name, contains match (case-insensitive)
Sort by: sequential, buyerName, issueDate, status. Defaults to created_at DESC when omitted.
Sort direction: asc, desc. Defaults to desc when sortBy is given without sortDir.

request.description: Lists documents for the specified issuer with optional filtering, sorting, and pagination. Defaults to creation date (newest first) when no sortBy is given.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /v1/issuers)

request.description: Retrieves document metadata by access key. Does not call the SRI — use Check Authorization for status updates.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /v1/issuers)

request.description: Submits the signed XML to the SRI reception service. Document moves to `RECEIVED` or `RETURNED`.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /v1/issuers)

request.description: Queries the SRI authorization service. Document moves to `AUTHORIZED` or `NOT_AUTHORIZED`. On authorization, an email with the RIDE PDF and XML is sent to the buyer.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /v1/issuers)

request.description: Corrects and re-signs a document in `RETURNED` or `NOT_AUTHORIZED` status. Keeps the original `accessKey`, `sequential`, and `issueDate`. Send again after rebuilding.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /v1/issuers)

request.description: Downloads the RIDE PDF for an `AUTHORIZED` document. Returns `application/pdf`.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /v1/issuers)

request.description: Downloads the authorization XML for an `AUTHORIZED` document, or the signed XML for earlier statuses. Returns `application/xml`.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /v1/issuers)

request.description: Returns the full audit trail for the document — every lifecycle transition, SRI call, and email event.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /v1/issuers)

request.description: Returns the sum of all AUTHORIZED credit notes already issued against this document (use the original invoice's access key, not a credit note's own key), plus the remaining balance. Only AUTHORIZED credit notes count — pending or rejected ones are excluded.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /v1/issuers)

query.description: Set to true to resend an email that was already successfully delivered

request.description: Retries the authorization email for a single document. Document must be `AUTHORIZED` with `email_status` of `PENDING` or `FAILED`. Add `?force=true` to resend an already-delivered email.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /v1/issuers)

request.description: Retries all `PENDING` and `FAILED` authorization emails across all documents for the specified issuer. Maximum 100 per call.

item.name: Catalogs
item.description: Reference data for SRI-defined codes. Use these to build valid invoice requests. Require a Bearer API key.

request.description: Returns buyer identification types (e.g. 04 = RUC, 05 = Cédula, 06 = Pasaporte). Use the `code` as `buyer.idType` on invoice creation.
Returns SRI payment method codes. Use the `code` as `payments[].method` on invoice creation.
Returns accepted SRI payment term units (e.g. dias, meses). Use the `code` as `payments[].termUnit` (with `payments[].term`) when sending invoices a plazos.
Returns SRI tax type codes (e.g. 2 = IVA, 3 = ICE, 5 = IRBPNR). Use the `code` as `taxes[].code` on invoice items.
Returns tax rate codes grouped by tax type. Use `taxCode` as `taxes[].code` and `rateCode` as `taxes[].rateCode` on invoice items.

item.name: Notifications
item.description:
Tenant-level alerts. Both read and unread are returned; use `readAt` to decide what to show. Use `?sinceId=<id>` for efficient catch-up polling — pass the highest id seen on the last request to receive only new notifications.

All requests require a Bearer API key.

header.description: Optional — filter to a specific issuer's notifications plus tenant-level ones. Omit for all-tenant view.

query.description: Optional — return only notifications with id > sinceId. Store the highest id seen and pass it on the next poll to catch up efficiently.

request.description:
Returns active (unexpired) notifications for the tenant, newest first. Both read and unread are included.

**Polling pattern:** call on a schedule (e.g. every 60 s). Use `?sinceId=<id>` to retrieve only notifications created after your last poll — store the highest `id` you've seen and pass it on the next request.

Optional `X-Issuer-Id` header restricts results to a specific issuer's notifications plus tenant-level ones (`issuerId: null`). Omit it for an all-tenant view.

Marks a notification as read at the tenant level. Call this only when every user with access to the notification has acknowledged it on the frontend side. After this call, the notification no longer contributes to `unreadCount` on future polls.

Copy a notification `id` from the List Notifications response into the `notification_id` variable.
Returns the full notification preference list for the tenant — one entry per notification type. Types the tenant has never explicitly configured default to `enabled: true` (opt-out model).

Bulk-upsert notification preferences for the tenant. Send only the types you want to change — unmentioned types are unchanged.

When `enabled` is `false` for a type, no new notifications of that type are created. Existing unread notifications remain and can still be marked as read.

Valid types: `DOCUMENT_AUTHORIZED`, `CERT_EXPIRING`, `CERT_EXPIRED`, `SRI_SUBMISSION_FAILED`, `EMAIL_DELIVERY_FAILED`, `QUOTA_WARNING`.

item.name: Webhooks
item.description:
Register HTTPS callback URLs to receive event notifications in near-real time. When a notification is created or updated, the API immediately POSTs a signed payload to every subscribed active endpoint.

**Secret handling:** the signing secret is returned only at registration. Store it immediately — it cannot be retrieved later. If lost, deregister and re-register the endpoint.

**Tier limits:** FREE: 1 endpoint, STARTER: 2, GROWTH: 5, BUSINESS: 10.

All requests require a Bearer API key.

request.description:
Registers a new webhook endpoint and returns the signing `secret` — **shown only once**. Store it immediately; it is used to verify `X-Comprobify-Signature` on incoming webhook requests.

`url` must be a valid HTTPS URL.

`eventTypes` is optional. Omit it or pass `[]` to subscribe to **all** event types. Valid values: `DOCUMENT_AUTHORIZED`, `CERT_EXPIRING`, `CERT_EXPIRED`, `SRI_SUBMISSION_FAILED`, `EMAIL_DELIVERY_FAILED`, `QUOTA_WARNING`.

Copy the returned `id` into the `webhook_id` collection variable.
Returns all active webhook endpoints for the tenant. Signing secrets are never included in list responses.

Updates a webhook endpoint's URL, event subscriptions, or active flag. All fields are optional — send only what you want to change.

Copy an endpoint `id` from the List Webhooks response into the `webhook_id` variable.

Soft-deletes a webhook endpoint (`active = false`). The endpoint stops receiving deliveries immediately. Past delivery records are preserved for audit purposes.

Copy an endpoint `id` from the List Webhooks response into the `webhook_id` variable.

item.name: Admin
item.description: Admin endpoints for managing tenants, issuers, and API keys. Require the `ADMIN_SECRET` as a Bearer token.

request.description:
Create a tenant for manual onboarding. Status is set to ACTIVE automatically — no email verification required.

Copy the returned `id` into the `tenant_id` collection variable.

exec: if (r.tenant?.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('tenant_id', String(r.tenant.id)); console.log('tenant_id set to', r.tenant.id); }

request.description: Activate or suspend a tenant. Accepted values: `ACTIVE`, `SUSPENDED`.
Manually activate a tenant's email when email delivery is not configured or for support purposes.

Admin override for tenant promotion — skips the ACTIVE status check (does not require verified email). One-way action.

`initialSequentials` is optional. If omitted, all active document types on all issuers start at sequential 1.

All sandbox API keys are revoked and replaced with production keys. The response includes all new production tokens — store them immediately.

Generates a new tenant-scoped API key. The plaintext token is returned only once — store it immediately.

`label` is optional (max 100 chars). `environment` is optional — omit it to default to the tenant's current active environment (`sandbox` or `production`), or pass `"sandbox"`/`"production"` explicitly to override.

Copy the returned key value into the `api_key` collection variable.

formdata.description: Numeric tenant id (required)
13-digit RUC
Optional trade name
Main (fiscal) address
Branch address (optional — defaults to mainAddress if omitted)
3-digit branch code
3-digit issue point code
Always 1 (normal emission)
true if the issuer is required to keep accounting (obligado a llevar contabilidad)
Special taxpayer resolution number (optional)
P12 certificate file
Optional. Document type codes to enable (default: ["01"])
Optional — pre-seed sequential counters. Example: [{"documentType":"01","sequential":500}] means the first invoice will be 000000500.

request.description:
Creates a new issuer for a tenant by uploading a P12 certificate. Requires `tenantId`. **Does NOT return an API key** — mint one separately via `POST /v1/admin/tenants/:id/api-keys`.

After a successful response, copy the issuer `id` into `issuer_id`.

formdata.description: Numeric tenant id (required)
ID of the issuer to copy the certificate from
Optional trade name
Main (fiscal) address
Branch address (optional — defaults to mainAddress if omitted)
Different branch code from the source issuer
true if the issuer is required to keep accounting
Special taxpayer resolution number (optional)
Optional — pre-seed sequential counters. Example: [{"documentType":"01","sequential":500}]

request.description: Creates a new branch issuer by copying the certificate from an existing issuer. No P12 file needed. Requires `tenantId`. **Does NOT return an API key** — mint one separately via `POST /v1/admin/tenants/:id/api-keys`.
Returns all active issuers across all tenants.

formdata.description: P12/PFX certificate file from an authorized Ecuadorian CA (BANCO CENTRAL or SECURITY DATA)
Optional. Password protecting the P12 file, if any.

request.description: Admin override — renews the P12 certificate for any issuer, no tenant ownership check. Uses the admin_secret as the Bearer token.
Revokes (soft-deletes) an API key by ID. The key stops working immediately.

exec: console.log('First pending payment_id set to', r.payments[0].id);
r.payments.forEach(p => console.log(' Payment', p.id, p.status, p.amount, 'USD — tenant', p.tenant?.email));

request.description: List payments awaiting manual review. Default status=REPORTED (proof submitted, decision pending). Captures payment_id of the first result.

exec: if (r.ok) console.log('Generated', r.generated, 'document instances for tenant {{tenant_id}}');

request.description: Generates personalized PENDING document instances for a tenant who registered before agreements were published, or to regenerate after a template update. Reads current published templates + the tenant's primary issuer data. Idempotent — skips types already generated for the current template version.

exec: if (r.document?.version) { pm.collectionVariables.set('agreement_version', r.document.version); console.log('Published TERMS v', r.document.version); }

request.description: Reads content from docs/legal/*.md on the server — no markdown in the body. Only documentType and version are required.
Reads content from docs/legal/*.md on the server — no markdown in the body. Only documentType and version are required.
Reads content from docs/legal/*.md on the server — no markdown in the body. Only documentType and version are required.

Runs all periodic notification jobs across every non-suspended tenant:

1. **Certificate expiry checks** — inspects `cert_expiry` for every active issuer and upserts `CERT_EXPIRING` / `CERT_EXPIRED` alerts. Auto-dismisses alerts when a cert has been renewed (> 30 days remaining).

2. **Webhook retry queue** — retries all `RETRYING` webhook deliveries past their scheduled `next_retry_at` time.

This endpoint is designed to be called by an external scheduler (cron, infrastructure job) on a regular interval — every 5 minutes is a reasonable default. The job is **idempotent**: running it multiple times is safe.

Response includes `tenantsChecked` and a `retries` breakdown (`attempted`, `succeeded`, `failed`, `exhausted`).

Runs two things, in this order (order matters — see below):

1. **Scheduled downgrades** — every subscription with a `pending_tier` whose `current_period_end` has passed: flips `tier` to the scheduled `pending_tier`, clears `pending_tier`, grants the new (lower) quota, and rolls `current_period_start`/`current_period_end` forward by one billing interval so it re-enters the renewal cycle below at its new tier (a downgrade owes no payment, but it still needs a fresh period).
2. **Renewals** — opens a `RENEWAL` payment + reminder (notification + email) for every ACTIVE subscription within 7 days of `current_period_end` that doesn't already have one open, then downgrades to FREE (with its own notification + email) any subscription more than 7 days past `current_period_end` with no renewal ever verified. This step must run after step 1 in the same call — a downgrade that just rolled its period forward would otherwise look identical to an expired renewal.

Upgrades apply immediately on invoice authorization and need no scheduled job — only downgrades and renewals wait for a date to pass.

Designed to be called by an external scheduler on a daily cadence (no need for the minute-level frequency the notification job uses). Idempotent — running it multiple times is safe; nothing due is simply skipped.

Response includes `applied` (downgrades), `remindersSent`, and `expired` (counts from this run).

Starts the manual subscription/payment pipeline for a tenant. `tier` must be STARTER, GROWTH, or BUSINESS (FREE never needs a subscription). `billingInterval` is optional (MONTHLY default, or YEARLY for the 2-months-free discounted price). Creates a `subscriptions` row (PENDING_PAYMENT) and a `payments` row (PENDING, amount taken from `priceMonthlyUsd`/`priceYearlyUsd` in `subscription-tiers.js`). Response includes `bankTransfer` info for the SPI transfer.

Fails with 409 SUBSCRIPTION_ALREADY_IN_FLIGHT if the tenant already has one in progress.

Copy the returned `subscription.id` and `payment.id` into the `subscription_id` / `payment_id` collection variables.
Lists every subscription a tenant has ever had, newest first.

Records your decision after checking the proof the tenant uploaded (Submit Payment Proof) against the bank. `decision` must be `VERIFIED` or `REJECTED`.

`rejectionReason` is **required when `decision` is `REJECTED`** — the tenant sees it via `GET /v1/subscriptions/me` and needs to know what to fix before re-uploading. Not used for `VERIFIED`.

`VERIFIED` moves the subscription to PAYMENT_RECEIVED — your next step is self-billing the invoice and Link Invoice to Subscription. `REJECTED` leaves the subscription at PENDING_PAYMENT; the tenant can submit a new proof for the *same* payment (Submit Payment Proof works again once REJECTED — it only refuses once a payment is VERIFIED).

Either decision fires a notification + email to the tenant automatically (PAYMENT_VERIFIED/PAYMENT_REJECTED) — covers initial subscriptions, tier changes, and renewals uniformly, so there's nothing extra to do here.

View the actual uploaded file first with Get Payment Proof.
Downloads the proof-of-transfer file the tenant uploaded via Submit Payment Proof. Returns 404 if nothing was uploaded yet.

Attaches the self-billed invoice (created the normal way via Create Invoice, using Comprobify's own issuer) to a subscription, by `accessKey` — the 49-digit SRI key, the same identifier every document response already exposes. `documents.id` is never returned anywhere, public or admin, so this couldn't take a numeric id. Moves the subscription to INVOICE_PROCESSING.

Once that invoice reaches AUTHORIZED (via the normal Send to SRI / Check Authorization flow), the subscription activates automatically — no further admin action needed.

exec: if (r.subscription?.status) console.log('Subscription status now:', r.subscription.status, '— awaiting SRI authorization');

request.description: Cancels a subscription at any point. Does not auto-downgrade the tenant's tier — no renewal/expiry logic exists yet.
Returns `{ status: "ok", uptime }` (200) when the database is reachable, or `{ status: "error", uptime }` (503) when it is not. Use for load balancer health checks and container liveness probes.
